rows.notes: Page title clearly written at top centre. Letters legible and no amounts present.
Left-hand group heading; handwriting is ornate and the second word is uncertain (appears as 'Oton' or 'Oron'). No amounts on this heading line.

rows.description: Strip of Ground in Coll. front
rows.notes: Descriptive line immediately under the group heading. Script is reasonably clear and there are no amounts on this particular line (sub-items follow).
The word 'Quit Rent' is readable and the amount '1 0 0' aligns with the first (pounds) column between the right ruling lines. Values validate as normal currency.
Description reads as 'Capons'. Amount '0 2 6' (2s 6d) is clearly aligned in the shillings/pence columns and validates.
Word 'Acquittance' is legible. Amount '0 0 8' (8d) sits in the pence column and is unambiguous.

Group total printed to the far right as '1 3 2'. No text label on the same line; taken as the subtotal for the 'Strip of Ground' group. The sum matches the sum of the three item lines (1.0.0 + 0.2.6 + 0.0.8 = 1.3.2). Alignment with the right ruling lines is clear.
Clear centered/indented heading introducing the next group. No amounts on this heading line.
Sub-item under 'New Buildings'. The label 'Quit Rent' is readable but the individual amount for this line is not clearly written or is obscured by a bracketed note; left blank to avoid misalignment.
Another sub-item; description legible ('Capon Money' or similar) but the small amounts for each sub-line are not distinctly entered or are obscured by manuscript marginal notes.
Sub-item word 'Acquittances' visible. Individual amount not clearly separated from surrounding hand-written marginal notes, so left blank.

Group total for 'New Buildings' is written on the right as '2 4 8'. The digits align with the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Some marginal notes overlap the central text, but the total digits are clear.
Short dated annotation beneath the 'New Buildings' subitems; handwriting is very unclear ('1/2 pr ye... 1850'). No amount is legible. Low confidence due to heavy abbreviation and ink density.

Description likely 'Corner Furnishings' (word uncertain: could be 'Corner Paintings' or similar). The amount at the right reads as '0 1 8' (1s 8d) and aligns well with the pence column; description uncertainty lowers confidence.

Name/office line (handwriting ambiguous: 'Aldis Pte Bursar'). The amount reads '18 4' in the pounds and shillings columns; the pence column appears blank/zero. The exact reading of the description is moderately uncertain.
Appears to refer to a College annual payment. Amount '4 12 6' is clear and aligns with the ruling lines. Description is partially abbreviated but legible.
College-related entry (abbreviations present). Amount '8 2 0' (8 pounds 2 shillings) is readable; some letters in the description are uncertain.
The text 'Coll. Balliol' is reasonably clear. Amount '12 6 0' aligns with the columns and is unambiguous.
Surname and abbreviation for 'Adjutant' are somewhat stylized ('Sherwison' / 'Sherwinson'); amount '10 0 0' is clear in the pounds column.
Label 'Janitor' is clear. Amount reads '1 14 8' and is well aligned with columns (1 pound, 14 shillings, 8 pence).
This line is a certificate/clerical payment entry; the descriptive abbreviation is difficult ('Publio Rev. ad J. Natis. 1849' is a best reading). The amount '1 6 8' is legible.

rows.description: Ed. ad G. D. Jo. Bapt. 1850
rows.notes: Another ecclesiastical note with abbreviation; text ambiguous. Amount '0 6 8' (6s 8d) visible in the shillings/pence columns.
Single-word entry 'Lidem' (likely 'idem' or 'vide idem'). Amount reads '0 2 0' (2s) though small and slightly faded; alignment supports reading.
Event-related line; script legible. Amount '2 2 0' clear and aligns correctly.
'Eidem' (idem) with date 'Pasch. 1850'. Amount '2 2 0' clearly shown; handwriting consistent with previous line.
Line refers to payments to vicars; description 'Vicars Newton ad St. Ann. 1850' is reasonably clear. Amount '40 0 0' is large and clearly written in the pounds column.
Similar 'Eidem' (same) entry for St. Michael 1850. Amount '40 0 0' is clear and aligns with the pounds column.

rows.description: Vicars P. Newton ad St. Ann. 1850 - P.R.D.
rows.notes: Detailed line referencing vicars and an abbreviation 'P.R.D.'; amount '38 16 8' is neatly written and clearly aligned. Some abbreviation letters in the description are stylized but legible.
Companion line to previous; amount '38 16 8' repeated and clear. Abbreviation 'Do.' (ditto) visible.

rows.description: Assesd Taxes for the Lodgings April 1850
rows.notes: Line explicitly reads 'Assesd Taxes for the Lodgings April 1850'. Pence reads '11' with a small half-penny marker (loop 'ob'), interpreted as 0.5. 6/11/11½ is consistent and columns align.
Repeat entry for October 1850. The pence again show '11' with the same half-penny flourish; interpreted as 11½d. Alignment with ruling lines is good.

rows.description: Land Tax for the City premises
rows.notes: Descriptive phrase 'Land Tax for the City premises' visible. Amount reads as '4 5 5' (4 pounds, 5 shillings, 5 pence); digits are smaller but align with the columns.
Line clearly reads 'River Water from City Works'. Amount '5 5 0' is neatly written and unambiguous.
Insurance entry is clearly described and the substantial amount '48 17 6' is crisply written and aligns exactly with the three right-hand ruling columns.
Repeat 'Do.' with a school name that appears as 'Heddington' (spelling uncertain). Amount looks like '1 10 0' but the pence area is faint—interpreted as zero pence.
Extended 'Do.' line referencing Heddington and a contractor/firm; handwriting of names is uncertain. Amount '9 7 3' visible though some digits are slightly cramped.
Final insurance/related 'Do.' line with date 1851. Proper name is unclear ('Littley' tentative). Amount '11 6 8' is legible in the columns.
Small closing line for a temporary sexton payment ('pro temp.'). Amount reads '1 16 0' though the description letters are cramped and somewhat uncertain.
Final page total in the extreme lower-right reads '280 0 1'. Clearly aligned with the right-hand ruling lines and legible; used as the main alignment reference for column placement.
